reason: This task PRD — 5 ADRs (Q1 V1 scope=C / Q2 location=C api-side / Q3 startStage=B DB column / Q4 auto-pick=A / Q5 rules-only=A), R1-R35 requirements, AC-1~AC-23, 4 PR breakdown, R-Risk-1~6
Always-included shared guides bundle
router is a NEW module that consumes Coordinator output — verify Coordinator (apps/runner/src/agents/coordinator/) stays unchanged; recommend() pure function fits existing service helper pattern

router types live in shared (RouterInput/RouterRecommendation); router logic in api; called by createWorkflowRun (api) + dry-run endpoint (api); UI calls /router/recommend (web → api). Triple-layer alignment crucial

Canonical FLOW_REGISTRY contracts — router decision rules R10-R13 select among the 4 registered FlowIds; W2-2b spec doc already enumerates all 4 flows + their stages; W2-4 appends § Smart Router section
W2-1 ADR Q1=α (kind/skillId placeholder no-consumption); R1-R3 zero-regression contract; W2-4 must NOT activate skillId routing
W2-3 thin extension reference; runtime degradation pattern when prefix stages skipped (fastforward already validates this for context_pack/requirement/design)
W2-2a Q3=C stage-history-aware acceptance gate (already shipped, W2-4 inherits); SkillSpec inputs.required relaxation pattern
W2-2b Q2=A WorkflowRunType extension precedent; W2-4 uses 'refactor' as router input via Coordinator (and fixes CoordinatorAction.runType literal mirror that W2-2b missed)
Wave 2 roadmap — W2-4 estimate (~2 weeks / ~4 PR / rule engine + UI 入口卡 + 历史模式存储 + 集成 / 测试); shared naming conventions
V2 design source — § 1.3 V1 process rigidity / § 2.1 work-type polymorphism / § 3.2 Routing over Prescribing (the philosophical anchor for W2-4) / § 4.2 first-phase 3 workflows / § 6 design-trap 4
RouterInput / RouterRecommendation in shared; WorkflowRun.startStage field; CoordinatorAction.runType mirror fix; KnowledgeArtifact-related types router uses for relevant_knowledge
TS quality bar — RouterRecommendation must be a strict interface (not Record); rulesFired array semantics mirrors Coordinator; FlowId / WorkflowStage union narrowing remains intact
runner backend spec index — orchestrator runWorkflow from-stage slice (R19); RunCtx unchanged; mustSkill no new mapping needed (router doesn't call skills)

runner quality bar — orchestrator slice(fromIdx) preserves dispatchStep _exhaustive: never; from-stage skip doesn't introduce flowId-aware branching in dispatchStep itself (still pure stage-name driven W2-1 ADR Q1=α)
Verify runWorkflow throws on `unknown startStage in flow` rather than silently falling back; same defensive pattern as W2-1 PR3 'unknown flowId in registry'

api backend spec index — router.ts in apps/api/src/; routes/router.ts new endpoint; createWorkflowRun is sole writer of workflow_runs table; store helpers used by router (store.knowledgeArtifacts.byProject etc)

api quality bar — router pure function (deterministic, no side effects); /router/recommend is read-only endpoint; sole-writer principle for workflow_runs preserved (router doesn't write, just recommends)
workflow_runs.start_stage migration follows W2-1 PR3 idempotent ALTER pattern (columnNames guard + UPDATE backfill if needed); nullable column with DEFAULT NULL

/router/recommend endpoint error responses — 400 for missing projectId; 500 unhandled passes through to global handler; rules-only path doesn't throw on classification ambiguity (returns lowest-priority default)
web/main.ts smart-router entry card UI conventions; fetch /router/recommend on title input blur; client-side caching by (projectId, title) hash
